item.title: Fed's Williams: FOMC leadership not in question amid slow Warsh confirmation
item.description: Federal Reserve Bank of New York President John Williams said on Tuesday that U.S. Federal Reserve ​Chair Jerome Powell would remain in charge of the central bank’s key monetary policy function even if Kevin Warsh ‌has not been confirmed as the new Fed leader by next month.

item.title: Hyundai resuming new Palisade SUV sales after finalizing fix
item.description: Hyundai Motor said Tuesday it ​is lifting a halt ‌on sales of recalled 2026 Palisade SUVs after ​finalizing a fix ​for a power seat ⁠issue that resulted ​in the death of ​a two-year-old girl in Ohio.

item.title: Gautam Adani will seek to dismiss US SEC fraud case
item.description: Gautam Adani, one of ​India's richest people, ‌will ask a judge to dismiss the U.S. ​Securities and Exchange ​Commission's civil fraud case ⁠against him, his ​lawyers said on Tuesday.

item.title: From falling U.S. wealth to Indian factory closures, oil shock raises global recession risk
item.description: Kevin Kelly is in a tough spot. What he says are unprecedented price increases in the weeks since the United States waged war on Iran mean the Californian, who makes plastic bags for groceries, may have to break contracts he cannot afford to honor with his customers.

item.title: USTR Greer says US-Mexico-Canada pact talks may run past July 1 deadline
item.description: The Trump administration will try to resolve as many problems with the ​U.S.-Mexico-Canada trade agreement as it can before July 1, ‌but negotiations to rebalance the trade pact are likely to continue past that deadline, U.S. Trade Representative Jamieson Greer said on Tuesday.

item.title: Deal, delay or strike? Investors on edge as Trump's Iran deadline nears
item.description: Global markets have entered a period of heightened uncertainty ahead of a deadline set by U.S. President Donald Trump for Iran, with investors weighing various outcomes ranging from a ceasefire to renewed military escalation and their implications for oil, currencies and risky assets.

item.title: Heads of IEA, IMF, World Bank to meet next Monday to discuss energy crisis
item.description: The leaders of the International Energy Agency, the International Monetary Fund and the World ​Bank will discuss the energy crisis triggered by ‌the Iran war next Monday, IEA executive director Fatih Birol said on Tuesday.

item.title: Vape makers turn to 'Made in America' credentials amid Trump's tariffs, crackdown
item.description: The U.S. vaping market, long dominated by Chinese imports, is seeing an uptick of "Made in America" products, in what some analysts and industry executives ​say is a reaction to the Trump administration's crackdown on unlicensed brands.

item.title: Southwest Airlines to limit passengers to one portable charger on flights
item.description: Southwest Airlines said Tuesday it will ​limit passengers to one ‌lithium portable charger on flights and ban them from ​being stowed in ​overhead bins to reduce risks ⁠of inflight battery ​fires.

item.title: US lawmakers press Bessent on LNG ships that are taking tax credit
item.description: A group of Democratic U.S. senators on Tuesday pressed Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent on whether enormous tankers ​that ship liquefied natural gas qualify for a lucrative tax ‌credit meant for smaller boats that burn the fuel.

item.title: NY Fed March survey finds jump in near-term inflation expectations
item.description: Americans, rattled by surging energy prices ​tied to war in the Middle East, are expecting higher near-term inflation and fresh challenges to their ‌personal financial situations, the Federal Reserve Bank of New York reported on Tuesday.

item.title: Physical oil prices hit record highs near $150 a barrel as Hormuz crisis worsens
item.description: European and Asian refiners are paying record high prices ‌of near $150 a barrel for some crude oil grades, far exceeding prices for paper futures, highlighting the worsening supply crisis from the U.S.-Israel war with Iran.

item.title: Hot money increasingly dominates emerging markets financing, raising risks, IMF says
item.description: Emerging market ‌nations now get the bulk of their foreign financing from the likes of hedge funds, pension funds and insurers, leaving them vulnerable to rapid outflows during crises, the International Monetary Fund said in a report.

item.title: Intel to join Musk's Terafab mega AI chip project
item.description: Intel ​said ‌on ​Tuesday ​it ⁠would ​join ​Elon Musk's ​Terafab ​AI chip ‌complex ⁠project ​along ​with ⁠SpaceX, ​Tesla ​and ⁠xAI.

item.title: Delta Air hikes checked baggage fees as jet fuel prices soar
item.description: Delta Air Lines said on Tuesday it will raise fees for checked bags on domestic and select short-haul international ​routes, as airlines seek to offset soaring jet fuel ‌costs linked to escalating tensions in the Middle East.

item.title: Moody's cuts outlook on US BDCs to 'negative' on redemption pressure, rising leverage
item.description: Moody's Ratings on Tuesday revised its outlook on U.S. business development companies to negative ​from stable, citing rising redemption pressures, higher leverage ‌and weakening access to funding markets.

item.title: Uber bets on Amazon's custom chips to boost AI efforts
item.description: Uber is using Amazon's custom chips to speed up ​computing and train artificial intelligence ‌models, the cloud giant said on Tuesday, as the ride-hailing ​firm seeks advanced hardware ​to handle growing digital workloads.

item.title: Fed's Williams says Middle East war will drive up inflation - Bloomberg
item.description: Federal Reserve Bank of ​New York President John ‌Williams said Tuesday the Middle East war energy shock will ​drive up overall ​inflation over the course of ⁠this year, while reiterating ​monetary policy is in ​the right place to deal with what happens in the economy.

item.title: US core capital goods orders increase in February
item.description: New orders for key U.S.-manufactured capital goods ​increased more than expected ‌in February, but data for the prior month was ​revised sharply lower, suggesting ​some moderation in business spending ⁠on equipment in the ​first quarter.

item.title: Russia says Middle East war disruption opens up new trade opportunities
item.description: Global supply disruptions caused by the ​war in the Middle East have opened up ‌new trade opportunities for Russia, but price stability in the domestic market remains a priority, Russian Prime Minister Mikhail Mishustin ​said on Tuesday.

item.title: Gilead to buy Germany's Tubulis for up to $5 billion to boost cancer pipeline
item.description: Gilead said on Tuesday it would acquire private Germany-based Tubulis GmbH for up ​to $5 billion, as the U.S. drugmaker looks to boost its ‌pipeline with a lucrative class of experimental cancer drugs referred to as "guided missiles".

item.title: Puig, Estee Lauder founding families to meet for combination talks, source says
item.description: The founding ‌families of the Spanish firm Puig , which owns such brands as Rabanne and Nina Ricci, and U.S. cosmetics giant Estee Lauder will meet this week in New York to negotiate the ​terms of a potential business combination, a person with knowledge of the ​talks said on Tuesday.

item.title: Novo Nordisk launches higher-dose Wegovy in the United States
item.description: Novo Nordisk said on Tuesday that it has made a higher-dose ​version of its blockbuster weight-loss drug ‌Wegovy available across the United States.

item.title: US stock futures inch lower as investors assess Mideast developments
item.description: U.S. stock index futures ticked lower on ​Tuesday as investors assessed comments that indicated the Middle East conflict could escalate, ahead of President Donald Trump's deadline ‌for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Fox to integrate Kalshi prediction market data across news platforms
item.description: Fox Corp said on Tuesday it will integrate Kalshi's ​prediction market data across its ‌news and streaming platforms, reflecting a broader trend among media organizations to ​use market-based probabilities to complement ​traditional reporting.

item.title: AI networking firm Aria Networks raises $125 million in funding
item.description: Aria Networks said on Tuesday it has raised $125 million in its first series funding round, as the startup ​seeks to develop its AI networking infrastructure to meet the ‌soaring demand for capacity amid the rapid adoption of artificial intelligence.

item.title: ASML shares fall on US Congress plan to further restrict China exports
item.description: Shares of computer chip equipment maker ASML dipped on Tuesday, the first trading day after U.S. lawmakers last week proposed a law that could further restrict the company's sales to ​China.

item.title: Morning Bid: Final countdown?
item.description: What matters in U.S. and global markets today

item.title: Some firms in Saudi extend work from home ahead of Iran ultimatum
item.description: Some Western and Saudi firms in Riyadh have extended work-from-home advisories this week, five people familiar with the matter ​said, amid concern over a looming U.S.-imposed deadline for Iran ‌to open the Strait of Hormuz or face "hell".

item.title: UBS lowers 2026 S&P 500 target on Middle East conflict risks
item.description: UBS Global Wealth Management trimmed its S&P 500 index target for 2026, pointing to sustained higher oil ​prices amid the ongoing Middle East conflict that could pressure U.S. ‌economic growth and inflation.
